title: [lichen_l2.c:328] peer_try_all_pubkeys modifies ctx then restores on error
description: Function: peer_try_all_pubkeys (static helper). Contract violation: The function modifies ctx->peer_pubkey and ctx->peer_eui64 during iteration (lines 345-346), then restores them on failure (lines 367-368, 373-375). If lichen_link_rx() crashes or throws an exception (not possible in C, but if called from C++ wrapper), the context would remain in a modified state. More importantly, if lichen_link_rx() has side effects beyond the output parameters, those aren't rolled back. The saved_out_len restoration (line 347, 369) is correct. Priority 3 - theoretical concern in exception-free C code.
close_reason: Documented safety: ctx is a local stack variable in caller, protected by rx_mutex, and discarded on thread abort
